title: erica scoparia subsp. azorica
intro: Erica scoparia subsp. azorica, also known as Azores heath, is a subspecies of Erica scoparia, a flowering plant native to the Azores Islands in the Atlantic Ocean. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as wounds and eczema. Erica scoparia subsp. azorica can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
